name: claude-hooks-session-lifecycle
tags: session, lifecycle, checkpoint, SqliteSaver, evict, session_id, langgraph_checkpoints, UPS, trim, cap
body:
Session state is stored in SqliteSaver at ~/.claude/langgraph_checkpoints.db keyed by session_id. State survives server restarts.

Checkpoint cap: `_trim_checkpoints()` runs on every UserPromptSubmit — keeps the 5 most recently active sessions (`_CHECKPOINT_SESSION_CAP=5`, raised from 2 on 2026-07-05 — task:d2c347ca — to reduce eviction thrash with 3+ concurrent Claude Code windows), evicts the rest. This ensures stale sessions never accumulate regardless of server uptime.

Stop fires every turn and must never evict checkpoints — doing so would wipe cross-turn state like prompt_tools and session_tools needed by gates.

**Why:** SessionEnd is unreliable (not guaranteed on crash/force-quit). UPS is the only hook that fires consistently every turn, so checkpoint hygiene lives there.
